question: Will the Miami Dolphins draft a quarterback in the first round of the 2026 NFL Draft?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team selects a quarterback in the first round of the 2026 NFL draft. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Which teams will draft a QB in the 1st round of the 2026 Pro Football draft?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team selects a quarterback in the first round of the 2026 NFL draft. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins high probabilities on QB-needy teams like the New York Giants and Las Vegas Raiders selecting quarterbacks in the first round of the 2026 NFL Draft, driven by Daniel Jones' recent benching amid the Giants' 2-7 slump and the Raiders' instability with Aidan O'Connell and Gardner Minshew. Tennessee Titans also factor prominently due to Will Levis' inconsistent play. The 2026 college QB class, headlined by prospects like Arch Manning and Julian Lewis, offers depth, but draft positions hinge on 2025 NFL records and the prior draft's outcomes. Free agency, trades, and surprise rookie breakthroughs add uncertainty to these projections.

question: ECB rate hike in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the European Central Bank’s (ECB) deposit facility rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the conclusion of the ECB's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 16-17, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the ECB has released its rate change decision following its December meeting. If, however, the ECB’s December meeting is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or the rate change decision for that meeting is otherwise unknown by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate increase has occurred, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the European Central Bank (https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/key_ecb_interest_rates/html/index.en.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: ECB rate hike in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the European Central Bank’s (ECB) deposit facility rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the conclusion of the ECB's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 16-17, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the ECB has released its rate change decision following its December meeting. If, however, the ECB’s December meeting is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or the rate change decision for that meeting is otherwise unknown by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate increase has occurred, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the European Central Bank (https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/key_ecb_interest_rates/html/index.en.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on an 80% implied probability for an ECB rate hike in 2026 reflects sticky Eurozone inflation at 2.2% in November 2024, robust wage growth exceeding 4%, and looming fiscal expansions amid Germany's snap election after coalition collapse and France's rising deficits. ECB officials, including President Lagarde, held rates steady in recent meetings while projecting cuts to neutral levels around 2% by mid-2025, but highlighted upside risks from services inflation and potential U.S. tariffs under a Trump administration. Markets via OIS futures anticipate a policy bottom in 2025 before reacceleration pressures, with the December 12 ECB decision as the next catalyst.

question: ECB rate cut in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the European Central Bank’s (ECB) deposit facility rate is decreased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the conclusion of the ECB's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 16-17, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the ECB has released its rate change decision following its December meeting. If, however, the ECB’s December meeting is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or the rate change decision for that meeting is otherwise unknown by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate decrease has occurred, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the European Central Bank (https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/key_ecb_interest_rates/html/index.en.html), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: ECB rate cut in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the European Central Bank’s (ECB) deposit facility rate is decreased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the conclusion of the ECB's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 16-17, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the ECB has released its rate change decision following its December meeting. If, however, the ECB’s December meeting is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or the rate change decision for that meeting is otherwise unknown by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate decrease has occurred, this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the European Central Bank (https://www.ecb.europa.eu/stats/policy_and_exchange_rates/key_ecb_interest_rates/html/index.en.html), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Eurozone inflation remains sticky above the ECB's 2% target, with core rates at 2.7% in November 2024, supporting trader consensus for no rate cut in 2026 at 77.5%. The ECB's December 12 deposit rate reduction to 3% marked its fourth cut of 2024, but President Lagarde emphasized data-dependent policy and potential pauses amid robust GDP growth (0.4% Q3) and strong PMIs signaling economic resilience. Wage pressures and fiscal loosening in Germany further bolster hawkish outlooks, with rate futures implying steady policy through 2026 after 2025 easing. Upcoming inflation releases and ECB meetings in January and March will test this view.

question: Will the All India Trinamool Congress (AITC) win the most seats in the 2026 Assam Legislative Assembly election?
description:
Legislative Assembly elections are to be scheduled to be held in Assam, India, in March–May 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Assam Legislative Assembly election.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Assam Legislative Assembly.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (eci.gov.in). If there are multiple reports, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the most Assembly Constituencies (AC).

events.title: Assam Legislative Assembly Election Winner
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: The BJP's commanding 92% implied probability in the Assam assembly election market stems from Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma's strong incumbency advantage, bolstered by infrastructure projects, flood management successes, and effective handling of demographic concerns like illegal immigration. Recent opinion polls, such as those from Lokniti-CSDS in late 2024, project the BJP-led NDA securing over 80 of 126 seats, reflecting robust organizational strength and voter loyalty in tribal and tea garden belts. Opposition fragmentation—Congress at 7.6%, AIUDF declining amid defections—weighs against challengers. Realistic threats include a surprise INDIA bloc consolidation, major anti-incumbency from economic pressures, or national political shifts ahead of 2026 polls.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Assam Legislative Assembly.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (eci.gov.in). If there are multiple reports, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the most Assembly Constituencies (AC).

eventMetadata.context_description: The BJP's commanding 92% implied probability in the Assam assembly election market stems from Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma's strong incumbency advantage, bolstered by infrastructure projects, flood management successes, and effective handling of demographic concerns like illegal immigration. Recent opinion polls, such as those from Lokniti-CSDS in late 2024, project the BJP-led NDA securing over 80 of 126 seats, reflecting robust organizational strength and voter loyalty in tribal and tea garden belts. Opposition fragmentation—Congress at 7.6%, AIUDF declining amid defections—weighs against challengers. Realistic threats include a surprise INDIA bloc consolidation, major anti-incumbency from economic pressures, or national political shifts ahead of 2026 polls.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors DMK at 73.5% to win the 2026 Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly election, driven by its incumbency under Chief Minister MK Stalin, robust welfare schemes like free bus travel and breakfast programs, and a stable alliance with Congress and others that secured a landslide in 2021. AIADMK trails at 16.8%, hampered by post-Jayalalithaa leadership splits under Edappadi K Palaniswami and failed NDA ties. TVK, led by actor Vijay, surges to 9.5% on anti-corruption rhetoric and mass rallies drawing youth support since its 2024 launch. Recent pre-poll surveys, such as those showing DMK leads of 10-15 points, and Vijay's membership drives reinforce these odds, though alliance shifts and economic factors could evolve before nominations.

question: Will the Indian National Congress (INC) win the most seats in the 2026 Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly election?
description:
Legislative Assembly elections are to be scheduled to be held in Tamil Nadu, India, in March–May 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly in the 2026 election.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. If that also results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (https://eci.gov.in). If multiple official reports differ, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the greatest number of Assembly Constituencies (ACs).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors AINRC at 46.5% implied probability to win the Puducherry Legislative Assembly election, driven by incumbent Chief Minister N. Rangasamy's strong regional appeal and the party's pivotal role in the ruling National Democratic Alliance with BJP, which secured both Lok Sabha seats in 2024. BJP trails at 19.6%, benefiting from the coalition but facing questions over independent strength. Opposition INC (10.5%) and DMK (9.5%) lag amid fragmented anti-incumbent votes, while BSP (8.2%) gains niche Dalit support. Recent developments include steady governance without major scandals and infrastructure projects boosting AINRC sentiment, though pre-poll alliances and voter turnout remain key uncertainties ahead of the 2026 contest.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors the Indian National Congress (INC)-led United Democratic Front (UDF) at 67% implied probability to secure the most seats in the Kerala Legislative Assembly election, driven by mounting anti-incumbency against the incumbent CPI(M)-led Left Democratic Front (LDF) after consecutive 2016 and 2021 wins. Recent opinion polls, including Mathrubhumi's survey projecting UDF at 72 seats versus LDF's 58, underscore this shift amid governance critiques and economic concerns. CPI(M) lingers at 29%, reflecting resilient base support but fading momentum, while BJP and minor parties like RSP, CPI, and IUML remain marginal at under 1% each due to limited Kerala traction. The 2026 poll date looms without major catalysts.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Kerala Legislative Assembly in the 2026 election.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. If that also results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Kerala Legislative Assembly.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (https://eci.gov.in). If multiple official reports differ, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the greatest number of Assembly Constituencies (ACs).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors the Indian National Congress (INC)-led United Democratic Front (UDF) at 67% implied probability to secure the most seats in the Kerala Legislative Assembly election, driven by mounting anti-incumbency against the incumbent CPI(M)-led Left Democratic Front (LDF) after consecutive 2016 and 2021 wins. Recent opinion polls, including Mathrubhumi's survey projecting UDF at 72 seats versus LDF's 58, underscore this shift amid governance critiques and economic concerns. CPI(M) lingers at 29%, reflecting resilient base support but fading momentum, while BJP and minor parties like RSP, CPI, and IUML remain marginal at under 1% each due to limited Kerala traction. The 2026 poll date looms without major catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices IUML seats in the 2026 Kerala assembly election tightly around its 2021 tally of 15, with 13–21 seats collectively above 90% implied probability, reflecting steady voter loyalty in Malabar strongholds like Malappuram and Kozhikode amid UDF coalition dynamics. No major opinion polls have emerged since the last election, where IUML held firm despite LDF's statewide sweep, keeping odds bunched as traders weigh UDF revival potential against LDF incumbency and BJP's rising Muslim outreach. Separation could arise from UDF seat-sharing negotiations, local body poll outcomes in late 2025, or shifts in Congress-IUML coordination, potentially boosting toward 22+ or dropping below 13.

description:

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: 2nd largest company end of March?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the second-largest company in the world by market cap on March 31, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts slightly toward Apple (51.5% implied probability) as the second-largest company by market capitalization at March 31 close, driven by its current edge over Alphabet amid a fiercely competitive race behind Microsoft’s unchallenged $3.1 trillion lead. As of late March, Apple’s $2.85 trillion valuation holds a slim $150 billion buffer versus Alphabet’s $2.70 trillion, fueled by resilient services revenue offsetting iPhone softness in China. Alphabet’s surge—up 18% year-to-date on AI-driven cloud and search dominance—has eroded the gap, with traders eyeing Nasdaq volatility and final-session flows as pivotal. NVIDIA’s fade to 0.4% odds reflects post-peak corrections, underscoring mega-cap pecking order risks in this high-stakes endgame.

question: Will Microsoft be the second-largest company in the world by market cap on March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to the second-largest company in the world by market cap on March 31, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: 2nd largest company end of March?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the second-largest company in the world by market cap on March 31, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts slightly toward Apple (51.5% implied probability) as the second-largest company by market capitalization at March 31 close, driven by its current edge over Alphabet amid a fiercely competitive race behind Microsoft’s unchallenged $3.1 trillion lead. As of late March, Apple’s $2.85 trillion valuation holds a slim $150 billion buffer versus Alphabet’s $2.70 trillion, fueled by resilient services revenue offsetting iPhone softness in China. Alphabet’s surge—up 18% year-to-date on AI-driven cloud and search dominance—has eroded the gap, with traders eyeing Nasdaq volatility and final-session flows as pivotal. NVIDIA’s fade to 0.4% odds reflects post-peak corrections, underscoring mega-cap pecking order risks in this high-stakes endgame.

question: Will NVIDIA be the third-largest company in the world by market cap on March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to the third-largest company in the world by market cap on March 31, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: 3rd largest company end of March?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the third-largest company in the world by market cap on March 31, 2026, as of market close.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price Apple at 49.5% and Alphabet at 47.5% implied probabilities for third-largest company by market cap as of March 29 close, reflecting a nail-biter race with mere billions separating them behind Microsoft and Nvidia's AI-fueled dominance. Nvidia's 220%+ YTD surge cemented its No. 2 spot above $2.2 trillion, sidelining it and others like Amazon. Apple's edge stems from resilient services revenue offsetting iPhone softness in China, while Alphabet's cloud growth and search moat drive catch-up momentum amid antitrust overhangs. Trader consensus hinges on final-session volatility, with oil prices a wildcard for Saudi Aramco's forex-adjusted cap.

question: Will Göztepe SK vs. Çaykur Rizespor end in a draw?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for January 19, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

events.title: Göztepe SK vs. Çaykur Rizespor
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Süper Lig game, scheduled for Monday, January 19, 2026 between Göztepe SK and Çaykur Rizespor.

eventMetadata.context_description: Göztepe SK's near-certain trader backing stems from their dominant home form at Gürsel Aksel Stadium, where they've won five straight Süper Lig matches, coupled with Çaykur Rizespor's dismal away record—winless in their last six road games and conceding heavily. Recent injury reports confirm Rizespor missing star forward Dal Vukanovic and midfielder Jonjo Shelvey, severely hampering their attack, while Göztepe boasts a fully fit squad riding momentum from a 3-1 midweek cup win. Head-to-head history favors Göztepe with three victories in the last four meetings. Realistic challenges include an early Rizespor red card reversal for Göztepe or extreme weather disrupting play, though such upsets remain low-probability given the form gap reflected in market pricing.

question: Puffpaw FDV above $50M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Puffpaw's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Puffpaw (https://www.puffpaw.xyz/) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will the Bank of Mexico announce a decrease at the March meeting?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the overnight interbank interest rate as a result of the monetary policy decision of the Bank of Mexico's March 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Mexico after its policy meeting scheduled for March 26, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of Mexico calendar: https://www.banxico.org.mx/viewers2/JSP/calendarioDifusion_es.jsp

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Mexico's statement for their March meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Bank of Mexico Decision in March?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the overnight interbank interest rate as a result of the monetary policy decision of the Bank of Mexico's March 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Mexico after its policy meeting scheduled for March 26, 2026, as listed on the official Bank of Mexico calendar: https://www.banxico.org.mx/viewers2/JSP/calendarioDifusion_es.jsp

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Mexico's statement for their March meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are split nearly evenly on the Bank of Mexico's March 21 decision, with a 50.5% implied probability for no change in the 11.25% target rate versus 48.5% for a decrease, reflecting heightened uncertainty amid cooling but sticky inflation. February CPI eased to 4.43% year-over-year—within Banxico's trajectory—bolstering cut bets after last month's 50-basis-point reduction, yet core inflation at 4.39% and robust GDP growth signal caution to avoid peso volatility tied to U.S. Fed policy. Election aftermath stability has eased risks, but hawkish minutes emphasize data-dependence, leaving trader capital balanced as the meeting nears with no major releases intervening.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Central Bank of Colombia after its March 31, 2026 policy meeting, as listed on the official Central Bank of Colombia calendar: https://www.banrep.gov.co/es/calendario-eventos.

This market may resolve as soon as the Central Bank of Colombia's statement for their March meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Central Bank of Colombia Decision in March?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the target for the monetary policy rate as a result of the Central Bank of Colombia's March 2026 meeting versus the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Central Bank of Colombia after its March 31, 2026 policy meeting, as listed on the official Central Bank of Colombia calendar: https://www.banrep.gov.co/es/calendario-eventos.

This market may resolve as soon as the Central Bank of Colombia's statement for their March meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors a rate increase by Colombia's central bank (Banco de la República) at 96% implied probability for the March decision, driven by persistent inflation exceeding the 3% target—January CPI printed at 8.35% year-over-year, with core measures showing limited disinflation amid peso depreciation and robust domestic demand. The benchmark rate stands at 12.75% following steady holds amid sticky price pressures, with traders pricing in a 25bps hike to combat imported inflation from a weakening COP/USD. Challenges include softer-than-expected February inflation data or dovish global cues from Fed pauses, but consensus anticipates tightening to anchor expectations ahead of the late-March meeting.

question: State of Siege declared in Chile by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a legally binding decree declaring a State of Siege (“Estado de Sitio”) in Chile enters into effect at any point between this market’s creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To count toward resolution, the corresponding decree must explicitly constitute a “State of Siege” as defined by Article 40 of the Chilean Constitution, i.e., it must not be rejected by the National Congress within five days of the President submitting the declaration (after which Congressional approval is deemed granted if no decision is issued).

Only declarations explicitly constituting an “Estado de Sitio” under Chile’s constitutional states of exception will count. If the legal name of the “Estado de Sitio” state of exception changes via constitutional reform but retains the same essential powers, it may qualify.

A declaration of a “State of Emergency” (Estado de Emergencia) or “State of Catastrophe” (Estado de Catástrofe) will not count.

If a region is under a State of Emergency and is subsequently upgraded to a State of Siege, the market resolves "Yes" once the State of Siege takes effect as described above.

A decree applying to any province or region of Chile will qualify.

The primary resolution source will be the Diario Oficial de la República de Chile (www.diariooficial.cl). However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' strong 83.5% consensus against a state of siege declaration in Chile by June 30 stems from the absence of widespread civil unrest or invasion threats that would justify this rare constitutional emergency measure, last invoked during the 2019 protests. President Gabriel Boric's government has managed rising crime and northern border migration with targeted military deployments and localized states of emergency, avoiding nationwide escalation. Recent developments, including stalled pension reforms and security policy debates, show no surge in protests or violence, reinforcing stability amid 2025 election preparations. This calm public order underpins the market's low-risk assessment.

question: Will US GDP growth in Q1 2026 be less than 1.0%?
description:
This market will resolve according to the seasonally adjusted and annualized GDP "Advance Estimate" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product

Note: data in the first available GDP report is labelled by the BEA as an "Advance Estimate". The data found in the advance estimate will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the release of the advance estimate will not be considered for this market's resolution.

If the advance estimate is not released, this market will resolve based on the first officially published figure for real GDP for the specified quarter (e.g., the ‘second’ or ‘third’ estimate, etc.), as reported by the BEA. If no official estimate is released by the date the next quarter's advanced estimate is scheduled to be published, this market will resolve based on the most recent previous figure released by the BEA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a modest acceleration in US GDP growth for Q1 2026, with implied probabilities tightly clustered around 2.0–3.5%—led by 2.5–3.0% at 22.3%, followed closely by 2.0–2.5% (20.5%) and ≥3.5% (19.5%). This competitive dynamic reflects post-election bets on Trump-era fiscal stimulus, including tax cuts and deregulation, potentially lifting growth above the Atlanta Fed's long-term 2% trend, amid recent Q3 2024's robust 2.8% print and anticipated Fed rate cuts. Key differentiators include tariff-induced inflation risks capping upside versus productivity gains from AI investments fueling stronger outcomes, with traders eyeing December jobs data and Q4 GDP nowcasts as pivotal catalysts amid inherent forecast uncertainty.

question: Zendaya confirmed pregnant by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Zendaya announces that she is pregnant between market creation, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only credible announcements will qualify. Pregnancy announcements that are not credible, for example jokes, will not suffice.    

The resolution source will be statements from Zendaya or her representatives; however, a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

question: Will Grigor Dimitrov win the 2026 Australian Open?
description:
The 2026 Australian Open is set to take place from January 18 - February 1, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Australian Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Australian Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Australian Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after February 28, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open (https://ausopen.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will Russia capture Lyman by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Lyman railroad station located on Vulytsya Pryvokzalʹna by March 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Train Station Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/lyman+train+station+zoom.png

Train Station Location in Lyman: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/lyman+train+station.jpeg

Lyman Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/lyman.jpeg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/zeVSXaeDH93mF2WPA

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

question: Will Marcus Carter be the Republican nominee for FL-09?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the FL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on August 18, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Thomas Chalifoux leads Polymarket trader consensus at 65% implied probability for the FL-09 Republican primary, driven by his top fundraising totals exceeding $500,000, strong internal polling showing a double-digit edge, and endorsements from local GOP figures like state Senator Dennis Baxley. Justin Story trails at 11% amid solid grassroots support but weaker financials, while Marcus Carter (8%) benefits from veteran appeal yet lags in visibility. Jorge Malavet (7%) and Howard Steven Rance (4.3%) remain longshots due to limited resources and name recognition. Recent catalysts include Chalifoux's July debate performance and a fresh NRCC ad buy boosting his momentum ahead of the August 20 primary; no major shifts in recent surveys.

question: Will Russia capture all of Vovchansk by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Vovchansk by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Vovchansk will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities such as "Vovchanski Khutory" with a darker grey line.  If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures Vovchansk, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Vovchansk Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Vovchansk.png 
Location on Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/pEumzu6kdCPQ2sYt7

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap is rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have advanced to within a few kilometers of Borova, a key village near Kupiansk in Ukraine's Kharkiv region, but have not yet entered the settlement amid ongoing intense fighting. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects cautious optimism for Russian progress, driven by recent gains in the area—including capture of nearby positions like Krokhmalne—bolstered by Moscow's manpower surges and drone strikes disrupting Ukrainian logistics. Ukrainian reinforcements and Western-supplied artillery have slowed the push, maintaining defensive lines. Upcoming factors include potential U.S. aid resumption post-election and winter weather, which could hinder offensives; markets price in a 40-60% implied probability of entry by the deadline, per skin-in-the-game bets, amid fluid frontline dynamics.

question: Will Russia capture Sumy by March 31, 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian cross-border incursions into Sumy Oblast have intensified in recent weeks, with sabotage groups and small assault units probing Ukrainian defenses near the border, yet no significant territorial gains toward capturing Sumy city itself. Ukrainian forces, bolstered by fortified positions and Western-supplied artillery, have repelled most advances, maintaining control amid reports of Russian troop buildups in adjacent Kursk region. Trader sentiment on Polymarket leans against a full capture by the deadline, reflecting the grinding attritional warfare on this northern front, where harsh winter conditions loom and potential U.S. aid packages could further solidify defenses. Key to watch: any escalation in Russian aerial strikes or manpower shifts from Donetsk.

question: Ventuals FDV above $100M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Ventual's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ventuals (https://x.com/ventuals) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows low implied probabilities for Russia entering major Ukrainian cities by June 30, driven by stalled advances in key frontline areas like Chasiv Yar and Vovchansk despite intensified assaults in Donetsk and Kharkiv oblasts. Russian forces secured Avdiivka in March but have made only incremental gains since, hampered by Ukrainian defenses bolstered by Western munitions and drone strikes disrupting logistics. No verified breakthroughs into urban centers have occurred amid ongoing attrition warfare. Upcoming NATO summit outcomes in Washington (June 24-26) could accelerate aid flows, potentially shifting momentum, while diplomatic signals from recent Zelenskyy-Putin rhetoric remain unconfirmed and unlikely to prompt ceasefires before the deadline.

events.title: Which cities will Russia enter by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of [the specified city or settlement] () by June 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the city or settlement is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows low implied probabilities for Russia entering major Ukrainian cities by June 30, driven by stalled advances in key frontline areas like Chasiv Yar and Vovchansk despite intensified assaults in Donetsk and Kharkiv oblasts. Russian forces secured Avdiivka in March but have made only incremental gains since, hampered by Ukrainian defenses bolstered by Western munitions and drone strikes disrupting logistics. No verified breakthroughs into urban centers have occurred amid ongoing attrition warfare. Upcoming NATO summit outcomes in Washington (June 24-26) could accelerate aid flows, potentially shifting momentum, while diplomatic signals from recent Zelenskyy-Putin rhetoric remain unconfirmed and unlikely to prompt ceasefires before the deadline.

question: Will the Reserve Bank of New Zealand decrease the official cash rate after the April decision?
description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the official cash rate (OCR) resulting from the Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s April monetary policy decision, relative to the level it was prior to this decision.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Reserve Bank of New Zealand after its April 7, 2026 monetary policy decision, as listed on the official Reserve Bank of New Zealand monetary policy schedule: https://www.rbnz.govt.nz/news-and-events/events#sort=%40eventstart%20ascending&f:@hierarchicalz95xsz120xatopictagnames=[Monetary%20policy]

This market may resolve as soon as the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's media release for their April 7, 2026 decision with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the official cash rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Reserve Bank of New Zealand decision in April?

series.title: Reserve Bank of New Zealand Decision

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders on Polymarket assign a 91% implied probability to the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) holding its Official Cash Rate (OCR) steady at 5.5% in its April 10 decision, reflecting broad consensus on a pause after 13 consecutive hikes amid cooling inflationary pressures. Key supports include Q4 2023 CPI at 4.7% year-over-year—down from peaks—with core measures like trimmed mean inflation easing toward the 1-3% target band, softening labor market data (unemployment at 4.3%), and RBNZ's February guidance signaling no rush to tighten further despite persistent services inflation. Upside risks to a hike (6.7% odds) could emerge from hotter-than-expected Q1 CPI on April 16 or renewed NZD depreciation, while cuts remain negligible absent recession signals.

question: Will Chennai Super Kings win the 2026 Indian Premier League?
description:
The 2026 Indian Premier League (IPL) T20 cricket competition is scheduled to take place from March 26 to May 31, 2026. 

This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 Indian Premier League. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 Indian Premier League based on the rules of the competition (e.g., they do not advance to the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 Indian Premier League season is permanently canceled or there is otherwise no winner declared by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be the IPL (https://www.iplt20.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2026 IPL Champion
events.description:

This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 Indian Premier League. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2026 Indian Premier League based on the rules of the competition (e.g., they do not advance to the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 Indian Premier League season is permanently canceled or there is otherwise no winner declared by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be the IPL (https://www.iplt20.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mumbai Indians lead trader consensus at 22.5% implied probability for the 2026 IPL title, buoyed by their elite retained core of Rohit Sharma, Suryakumar Yadav, Hardik Pandya, and Jasprit Bumrah, offering batting firepower and death-over bowling edge ahead of the November 2025 mega auction. Yet the market stays competitive with Royal Challengers Bengaluru (15.5%) and Chennai Super Kings (13.5%) close behind, reflecting retention strengths like Virat Kohli's anchor role for RCB and CSK's blend of MS Dhoni's leadership, Ravindra Jadeja's all-round prowess, and Ruturaj Gaikwad's form. The upcoming auction's massive purse reset—over 100 crores per franchise—fuels parity, as Gujarat Titans, Punjab Kings, and Sunrisers Hyderabad could rebuild explosively with young guns like Shubman Gill or Pat Cummins, underscoring IPL's inherent volatility where historical five-time champs MI and CSK face hungry rebuilds.

question: Will Playboi Carti release an album in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed artist officially releases a new album between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM PT (Los Angeles time). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including recordings of live events) by the resolution date.

Album releases including non-original tracks such as live-versions, re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums, or similar non original or altered versions of previously released songs will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The primary resolution sources will be official streaming and download platforms. In cases of ambiguity over how a song/album is labeled, the listed resolution sources will be consulted in the following order of precedence: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and Deezer.

events.title: Which artists will release new albums in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed artist officially releases a new album between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM PT (Los Angeles time). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on 2026 album releases tilts toward established superstars like Taylor Swift and Beyoncé, with implied probabilities hovering around 65% and 50% respectively, fueled by their predictable release cycles following massive tours and recent hits like The Tortured Poets Department and Cowboy Carter. Recent interviews hint at Swift wrapping re-records by late 2025, positioning her for new material, while Beyoncé's Renaissance Act II buzz sustains optimism despite no firm dates. Industry dynamics favor quick turnarounds for streaming-era dominance, but delays from production or legal issues (e.g., catalog disputes) pose risks. Watch for announcements around Grammys in February 2026 or year-end teases, as official confirmations could swing odds sharply.

question: Will Morgan Stanley or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Goldman Sachs at 49.5% implied probability as lead underwriter for a potential SpaceX IPO, with Morgan Stanley close behind at 37%, reflecting their pivotal roles in the company's recent $350 billion valuation tender offer concluded in late November 2024, where both banks facilitated secondary share sales at $185 per share. These incumbents benefit from deep ties—Morgan Stanley's history advising Elon Musk on Tesla's 2010 IPO and multiple SpaceX debt rounds, including a $1.9 billion raise in 2023, while Goldman Sachs led that debt issuance. Lower odds for banks like BofA or JPMorgan stem from thinner SpaceX relationships amid no firm IPO timeline, though Starlink spin-off speculation persists into 2025.

question: Gensyn FDV above $400M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Gensyn's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Gensyn (https://x.com/gensynai) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will Trump recognize Somaliland before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally recognizes the Republic of Somaliland as a sovereign state by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only formal recognition of Somaliland will qualify. Statements/actions which acknowledge the Republic of Somaliland, or change its diplomatic status with the U.S. but do not formally recognize the country as a sovereign state will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Trump recognize Somaliland before 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 78.4% implied probability to no U.S. recognition of Somaliland by President Trump before 2027, primarily due to the absence of any official statements, transition team signals, or policy pledges from Trump indicating intent to break with longstanding U.S. precedent favoring Somalia's territorial integrity. Somaliland's recent diplomatic push, including its January 2024 memorandum with Ethiopia for Red Sea port access amid tensions with Mogadishu, has not prompted U.S. action, as Washington prioritizes Horn of Africa stability and counterterrorism partnerships over unilateral moves lacking NATO or African Union support. No campaign trail mentions or post-election developments have altered this cautious stance, underscoring trader confidence in inertia.

question: Will Gold (GC) settle at <$3,800 in June?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Gold futures on the final trading day of June 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official settlement price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution. If no settlement price is published for that session, the market will use the most recent published settlement for the Active Month during June.

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for the relevant trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

The razor-thin lead in upper-mid bins underscores competitive dynamics hinging on November FOMC decisions, CPI releases, and potential Trump tariff policies inflating commodity demand; sub-$4,000 odds lag at 20.9% combined, pricing in persistent upside momentum over recession risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on gold futures (GC) prices by end-June tilts bullish, with Polymarket odds implying a 65% chance of surpassing $2,400/oz, driven primarily by Federal Reserve rate cut expectations following softer May CPI data showing core inflation at 3.4% year-over-year. Current GC front-month trades near $2,330/oz, up 15% YTD amid a weakening U.S. dollar (DXY at 104.5) and persistent central bank buying, including China's recent 5-tonne addition. Key risks include the June 12 CPI release and FOMC meeting (June 11-12), where dot-plot revisions could strengthen the dollar if cuts are deferred; historical precedent shows gold rallying 5-10% post-pigeonhole Fed signals, though renewed risk-on flows may cap gains.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders show fragmented sentiment on silver (SI) June settlement, with a slim 16% implied probability for <$50 oz leading $60-$70 (14.6%) and $50-$60 (13.9%), reflecting competitive dynamics between industrial demand tailwinds and macroeconomic headwinds. Spot silver holds above $31/oz after a 25% YTD rally fueled by solar PV growth and Fed rate cut bets, but June futures near $32.10 signal modest contango amid USD resilience and steady mine supply. Key differentiators include Thursday's CPI release—hotter prints could propel $50+ bins via inflation-hedge flows—and FOMC signals on policy path, with China stimulus upside versus election volatility tilting trader capital across bins.

question: Will Silver (SI) settle at >$115 in June?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Silver futures on the final trading day of June 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official settlement price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution. If no settlement price is published for that session, the market will use the most recent published settlement for the Active Month during June.

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for the relevant trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing a modest implied probability for silver futures (SI) reaching $30 by June 30, reflecting proximity to current spot levels around $29.50 amid cooling inflation and Fed rate cut bets boosting precious metals. Primary drivers include a weakening U.S. dollar index below 105 and surging industrial demand from solar panel production, up 25% YoY per World Silver Survey data. However, hawkish FOMC rhetoric from the June 11-12 meeting and upcoming CPI release on June 12 could cap gains if inflation surprises higher. Historical precedent shows silver often rallies into summer on green energy tailwinds, but trader consensus hedges against renewed dollar strength.

question: Will Silver (SI) settle over $140 on the final trading day of June 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Silver futures on the final trading day of June 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices silver (SI) futures above $32 per ounce by June 30 at roughly 55% implied probability, driven primarily by surging industrial demand from solar panel production and electronics amid global green energy transitions, which accounts for over 50% of annual silver consumption per World Silver Survey data. Historical June seasonality shows modest 2% average gains.
